candidates.text:
New Anthropic research: Natural Language Autoencoders.

Models like Claude talk in words but think in numbers. The numbers—called activations—encode Claude’s thoughts, but not in a language we can read.

Here, we train Claude to translate its activations into human-readable text.

I turned Claude into a self-improving PM system.

Not by writing better prompts.
By giving it a way to learn.
Most AI memory is passive.
It stores context, but it doesn’t evolve ideas over time.

There’s no system to:
• validate patterns
• track recurring insights
• upgrade
Why do you lose interest in things you can't have?

Most wealth individuals work with some who does their taxes

But they don't have someone that does their "tax planning" 

Taxes are one of the most controllable parts of your financial life

You need filing and you need planning

A metal fab shop spent $60K on "AI quoting software."

Six months in, nobody trusted the output because it didn't know their actual steel suppliers or scrap rates.

We rebuilt it with a 300-line script that reads their real vendor sheets and historical job costs.

Same accuracy.

The "AI is replacing jobs" debate is misframed at the SMB level.

What's actually happening at the 20–200 person companies we work with: one person now runs the work that used to take three, because the boring 60% got automated.

Nobody got fired. They got redeployed to

A service company had 3 people manually routing incoming job tickets across 4 teams. Built them a simple intake form + classifier over a weekend.

Routing time: 45 min → 90 sec. Accuracy up ~22%.

Total stack: a Google Form, 47 lines of Python, $200/mo in API costs.
